tasks.notes: Titolo: Show HN: SammaPix – Free in-browser image compressor with AI rename
tasks.content:
Show HN: SammaPix – Free in-browser image compressor with AI rename

Hi HN! I built SammaPix (https://sammapix.com) — a browser-based image optimizer that:

- Batch compresses up to 20 images at once
- Converts to WebP
- Uses Google Gemini to rename files semantically (IMG_1234.jpg → product-hero-blue-sneakers.webp)
- 100% client-side — nothing is uploaded to any server
- Free forever, no account required for compression

The AI rename feature requires a free account (I needed some rate limiting). Everything else works without signing up.

Tech stack: Next.js 14 App Router, browser-image-compression library, Google Gemini 2.5 Flash for vision, Canvas API for format conversion.

Happy to answer any questions about the implementation!

tasks.notes: NON mostrare il signup. URL: sammapix.com/?ref=iib
tasks.content:
This website compresses, converts, and AI-renames your images — entirely in your browser

Just found SammaPix and it's genuinely impressive. You drag in a bunch of photos, it compresses them, converts to WebP if you want, and uses AI to rename them from IMG_1234.jpg to something actually descriptive like 'sunset-over-mountains.webp'.

The wild part: nothing gets uploaded. It all happens in your browser. No accounts, no waiting, no privacy concerns.

https://sammapix.com/?ref=iib

I built SammaPix — a free browser image optimizer with AI rename. Here's what I learned.

I built this because I kept getting frustrated renaming product photos manually. IMG_4523.jpg tells you nothing. I wanted something that could look at the image and give it a real name.

So I built SammaPix (https://sammapix.com):
- Batch image compression (up to 20 images)
- WebP conversion
- AI rename using Google Gemini Vision
- 100% client-side, nothing uploaded

Biggest technical challenge: keeping everything in the browser while still calling Gemini API (the API call goes through a Next.js route to protect the key, but the image data itself never touches a server).

Would love feedback from fellow builders!

tasks.notes: Angolo tecnico: Next.js + Gemini + Canvas API
tasks.content:
How I used Canvas API + Google Gemini to build a client-side image optimizer with AI rename [Next.js 14]

Built SammaPix (https://sammapix.com) — here's the technical breakdown:

**Compression**: browser-image-compression library runs entirely in a Web Worker. No WASM, no server.

**WebP conversion**: Canvas API toBlob() with 'image/webp' mime type. Simple but effective.

**AI Rename**: The tricky part. I send a base64 thumbnail (300px max) to Gemini 2.5 Flash via a Next.js API route. The route adds auth + rate limiting. The full image never leaves the browser.

**State management**: Zustand with immer for the file queue. Each file goes through states: queued → compressing → done/error.

Stack: Next.js 14 App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind, Zustand, NextAuth v4

Live: https://sammapix.com
Happy to discuss any implementation details!
